candidate_problems.text: What exactly is the minimal boundary-condition asymmetry required to jointly generate thermodynamic irreversibility, stable records, memory asymmetry, retarded radiation, and the preparation→measurement structure? In other words, which elements of the low-entropy past hypothesis are genuinely load-bearing, and which are replaceable by weaker or different constraints?

Why did the universe have a low-entropy beginning at all, and can that fact be explained non-circularly rather than merely postulated? This remains open because the conjecture relocates the arrow into boundary conditions but does not explain the origin, selection, or necessity of those conditions.

Is the claimed unity of the various arrows of time actually necessary, or could some arrows come apart under alternative cosmological or statistical setups? The open problem is to determine whether records, causation, radiation asymmetry, decoherence, and thermodynamic increase are structurally coupled by one boundary asymmetry or only contingently correlated in our universe.
